design_philosophy.core_loop: Infiltrate stages by taking cover behind doors and obstacles, eliminate enemy waves with escalating patterns, advance to story progression cutscenes showing captured partner's torture
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (ammo types and limited hits) combined with exposure risk when moving between cover points; instant-death projectile threats create high stakes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Secret agent conducting covert rescue mission with tactical positioning and gunplay against organized terrorist threats
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes cover-based positioning as core mechanic rather than reflexive dodging; two-hit life system with instant-death from projectiles creates stark high-consequence gameplay; story progression locked behind stage completion with escalating difficulty in Story 2

difficulty.lives_system: Two physical hits from melee enemies drain half life meter; instant death from projectile fire, lasers, time expiration, or floor falls
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; no mid-stage checkpoints; password system in NES version allows resuming from completed stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Arcade version offers DIP switch stage skip options allowing players to start at any point in Story 1; NES password system enables progression pacing control

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon substitution system: standard pistol can be replaced with submachine gun (Beretta M12-style); ammo depletion reverts to slower single-shot mode; ammunition found in marked doors
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story 2 (five harder stages with increased traps and repositioned enemies) unlocked by completing Story 1; NES version gates Story 2 behind password system
replayability_hooks: Story 2 difficulty escalation with altered enemy placement
Hidden bonuses accessible in NES version
Weapon and ammo loadout variations affecting stage approach
DIP switch stage selection in arcade allowing non-linear arcade play

world_building_techniques: In-game monitor screens displaying Leila's torture scenes at stage conclusions
Enemy faction visual identity: hooded Maskers with color/outfit variants indicating strength hierarchy
Location-based stage design (New York setting) with environmental enemy types (lava men, gelzos)

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation boss represents narrative antagonist (Geldra leader Maboo) encountered only at game completion; boss represents mission objective culmination
notable_bosses: Maboo - Geldra leader; encountered at end of final stage; mission-critical encounter

faction_indicators: Geldra - secret society terrorist organization antagonists
WCPO (World Crime Police Organization) - player character Albatross faction
Masker soldiers - color/outfit variants indicating rank and combat specialization
danger_signals: Instant-death projectile threats (grenades, lasers) creating high lethality environment
Time pressure mechanics forcing stage completion
Enemy variety escalation in Story 2 with trap increase suggesting organized opposition capability
environmental_storytelling: Cover-based stage architecture (doors, crates, multilevel rails) depicting infiltration setting
Enemy type distribution (ninjas, mutated creatures, lava men) suggesting diverse threat nature
Ammunition cache locations (marked doors) implying pre-planned supply routes

sources_used: tcrf - Rolling Thunder (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
